prompt: Eye-level wide-ish view of a budget kitchen refresh in a 1990s Haverhill split-level. Original oak cabinets repainted in a warm white, sanded-edge wear left honest. Polished-brass bar pulls replace the old wood knobs. Counters are thick edge-grain butcher-block in maple with a shallow waterfall on the corner. A worn but functional white farm sink. White penny-tile backsplash, simple. Vinyl plank floor in a mid-tone oak. Shot with a 35mm prime, late afternoon golden-hour sun beams through a side window casting strong slanting bars of warm light across the cabinet faces. A red Milwaukee impact driver rests on the counter. Slightly desaturated except for the brand red. NOT HGTV bright, NOT luxury staged, working-contractor catalog feel. No readable text, no logos.
